children.heading: .—Offenses involving actual or perceived race, color, religion, or national origin
children.label: 1 .—Offenses involving actual or perceived race, color, religion, or national origin

children.text: shall be imprisoned not more than 10 years, fined in accordance with this title, or both; and

children.heading: Offenses involving actual or perceived religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability.—
children.label: 2 Offenses involving actual or perceived religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability.—
i
children.text: shall be imprisoned not more than 10 years, fined in accordance with this title, or both; and

children.label: I
children.text: death results from the offense; or

children.label: II

children.label: I
children.text: across a State line or national border; or

children.label: II
children.text: using a channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce;

children.label: ii
children.text: the defendant uses a channel, facility, or instrumentality of interstate or foreign commerce in connection with the conduct described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: iii
children.text: in connection with the conduct described in subparagraph (A), the defendant employs a firearm, dangerous weapon, explosive or incendiary device, or other weapon that has traveled in interstate or foreign commerce; or

children.label: I
children.text: interferes with commercial or other economic activity in which the victim is engaged at the time of the conduct; or

children.label: II
children.text: otherwise affects interstate or foreign commerce.

children.heading: .—Offenses occurring in the special maritime or territorial jurisdiction of the united states
children.label: 3 .—Offenses occurring in the special maritime or territorial jurisdiction of the united states
children.text: Whoever, within the special maritime or territorial jurisdiction of the United States, engages in conduct described in paragraph (1) or in paragraph (2)(A) (without regard to whether that conduct occurred in a circumstance described in paragraph (2)(B)) shall be subject to the same penalties as prescribed in those paragraphs.

children.heading: .—Guidelines
children.label: 4 .—Guidelines
children.text: All prosecutions conducted by the United States under this section shall be undertaken pursuant to guidelines issued by the Attorney General, or the designee of the Attorney General, to be included in the United States Attorneys’ Manual that shall establish neutral and objective criteria for determining whether a crime was committed because of the actual or perceived status of any person.

children.heading: .—Lynching
children.label: 5 .—Lynching
children.text: Whoever conspires to commit any offense under paragraph (1), (2), or (3) shall, if death or serious bodily injury (as defined in ) results from the offense, be imprisoned for not more than 30 years, fined in accordance with this title, or both.section 2246 of this title

children.heading: .—Other conspiracies
children.label: 6 .—Other conspiracies
children.text: Whoever conspires to commit any offense under paragraph (1), (2), or (3) shall, if death or serious bodily injury (as defined in ) results from the offense, or if the offense includes kidnapping or an attempt to kidnap, aggravated sexual abuse or an attempt to commit aggravated sexual abuse, or an attempt to kill, be imprisoned for not more than 30 years, fined in accordance with this title, or both.section 2246 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the State does not have jurisdiction;

children.label: B
children.text: the State has requested that the Federal Government assume jurisdiction;

children.label: C
children.text: the verdict or sentence obtained pursuant to State charges left demonstratively unvindicated the Federal interest in eradicating bias-motivated violence; or

children.label: D
children.text: a prosecution by the United States is in the public interest and necessary to secure substantial justice.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction
children.label: 2 .—Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to limit the authority of Federal officers, or a Federal grand jury, to investigate possible violations of this section.
the term “bodily injury” has the meaning given such term in , but does not include solely emotional or psychological harm to the victim;section 1365(h)(4) of this title
the term “explosive or incendiary device” has the meaning given such term in ;section 232 of this title
the term “firearm” has the meaning given such term in ;section 921(a) of this title
the term “gender identity” means actual or perceived gender-related characteristics; and
the term “State” includes the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, and any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: .—Offenses not resulting in death
children.label: 1 .—Offenses not resulting in death
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), no person shall be prosecuted, tried, or punished for any offense under this section unless the indictment for such offense is found, or the information for such offense is instituted, not later than 7 years after the date on which the offense was committed.

children.heading: .—Death resulting offenses
children.label: 2 .—Death resulting offenses
children.text: An indictment or information alleging that an offense under this section resulted in death may be found or instituted at any time without limitation.

children.heading: .—Supervised Release
children.label: e .—Supervised Release
children.text: If a court includes, as a part of a sentence of imprisonment imposed for a violation of subsection (a), a requirement that the defendant be placed on a term of supervised release after imprisonment under section 3583, the court may order, as an explicit condition of supervised release, that the defendant undertake educational classes or community service directly related to the community harmed by the defendant’s offense.
